conditions: Gold Bricks are a type of brick crafted from Gold Ore . In some worlds, they are found as part of Jungle Shrines in the Underground Jungle . Like the ore they are made of, placed Gold Bricks will sparkle.

The Gold Brick Wall is a type of background wall which can be found naturally generated in Jungle Shrines in some worlds. It drops as an item when destroyed, and can also be obtained via crafting. It is always considered a safe wall .

The Gold Chest is a naturally-generated chest , with various loot as its contents. Each chest includes one special item, as well as some coins and other more common items. Gold Chests are found in Underground Cabins in the Underground and Caverns , in the Pyramids .

notes: Gold Dust is a Hardmode crafting material that is purchased from the Merchant for 17 SC each. Its sole purpose is to craft the Flask of Gold and Golden Bullets , which both allow the player to inflict the Midas debuff .

conditions: Golden Carp are a type of fish which are found extremely rarely by fishing in any biome in the Underground , Cavern and Underworld layers, with the exception of the Desert . They have an extraordinarily high sell value of 10 GC .
Gold Ore is a pre-Hardmode ore . It spawns in the Underground and Cavern layers , and occasionally on Floating Islands . Gold Ore also has a small chance to appear as a bonus drop from slimes .

notes: The Golf Cart Keys are a post- Skeletron mount-summoning item that places the player in a rideable Golf Cart . The Golf Cart emits light from its front that reaches about 40 tiles, with the brightest point being at about 15 tiles. The Golf Cart Keys can be purchased from the Golfer for 50 GC after Skeletron has been defeated, and a golf score of 2000 is earned by the player.

The Golf Cup is a block and activation mechanism used in Golf . It can be purchased from the Golfer for 1 GC . When a Golf Ball enters the cup, the Golf Ball projectile is removed from the world, the cup emits a wire signal, and a status message will share how many strokes the player took between placing the ball, hitting it and reaching the cup.
The Golf Tee is a furniture item used in golfing purchased from the Golfer for 1 GC . It assists in placement of Golf Balls , and balls struck from a Golf Tee will travel further.

The Golf Whistle is a tool used in golf that allows a player to recover Golf Balls , returning it to the previous position before it was hit when used. Using the Golf Whistle adds one stroke to the player's golf score for the shot as a penalty. The Golf Whistle can be purchased from the Golfer for 10 GC .
The Gradient is a Hardmode yoyo that can be purchased from the Skeleton Merchant for 20 GC during moon phases 1–4.

conditions: Granite is a block found underground in Granite Caves . In addition to being found in placed form, it can also drop from Granite Elementals and Granite Golems .
Smooth Granite Blocks are blocks with a dark blue appearance. The block can be crafted or found as walls in underground houses in Granite Caves . They can be used to craft the Granite furniture set.

The Granite Chest is a chest storage item found only in Underground Cabins within Granite Caves . Like Marble Chests and Rich Mahogany Chests , Granite Chests have exactly the same loot as Gold Chests .

The Granite Wall is a type of background wall which can be found naturally generated in Granite Caves , considered unsafe . It does not drop as an item when destroyed. It can also be generated as part of an Underground Cabin in a Granite Cave, considered safe . It is also considered a safe wall when placed by the player.

notes: Grass Seeds are seeds used to plant regular grass on Dirt Blocks . They are purchased from the Dryad for 20 CC each, while a Blood Moon is not occurring. They can also be obtained from putting Poo into an Extractinator .

conditions: The Grass Wall is a type of background wall which can be found naturally generated in above-ground tunnels and grottoes on the surface , considered unsafe . It does not drop as an item when destroyed, and can only be obtained by purchasing it from the Dryad for 10 CC each, while a Blood Moon is not occurring. It is considered a safe wall when placed by the player.
Greater Healing Potions can be crafted using materials found in The Hallow or obtained from most Hardmode bosses as well as from Biome Mimics and Martian Saucers .

notes: The Greater Mana Potion is a Hardmode recovery potion that restores 200 mana and inflicts the Mana Sickness debuff upon use. Unlike most potions, Greater Mana Potions cannot be crafted, and are instead purchased from the Wizard for 5 SC , or dropped from all Biome Mimics except the Jungle Mimic .

conditions: The Grebe is a critter that spawns in bodies of water in the Desert biome that are directly above Sand Blocks . It may turn hostile to players with negative luck ; see hostile critter for details . The Grebe has only 5 health and no defense , and can be damaged by other enemies and players.

quantityText: Green Cap (Only if name is Andrew)

conditions: The Green Mushroom is a type of mushroom . It grows on Dirt , Stone , Mud , or regular Jungle grass in the Underground and Cavern layers. [ 1 ] Unlike many plants, instead of broken by any attack, it has to be harvested with a pickaxe . Its sole purpose is to make Green Dye .

notes: The Grenade is an explosive consumable ranged weapon which can be purchased from the Demolitionist for 75 CC each, or found rarely in pots and natural Chests . Once Hardmode is activated, they can be found in Pots (replacing Shurikens ).

quantityText: Grenade Launcher + Rocket I (50–149) (will always drop the first time Plantera is defeated in a world that is neither in Expert nor Master Mode)

notes: The Guy Fawkes Mask is a head piece vanity item . It is purchased from the Clothier during Halloween for 3 GC .

The Mystic Robe is an armor item that can be worn in the shirt slot. It provides minimal defense (2) but a 6% damage and critical strike boost to magic weapons , along with a 10% reduction in mana use. It is purchased from the Traveling Merchant for 3 GC 50 SC .

conditions: The Hallowed Crate is a crate that can only be fished in The Hallow . It contains items found in standard crates.

The Divine Crate is a Hardmode crate that can only be fished in The Hallow . Divine Crates contain items found in standard crates, and additionally have a chance to drop Crystal Shards or Souls of Light . Its pre-Hardmode equivalent is the Hallowed Crate , and its Corruption and Crimson variants are the Defiled Crate and Hematic Crate , respectively.

The Hallowed Grass Wall is a type of background wall which can be found in The Hallow , considered unsafe . It does not drop as an item when destroyed, and can only be obtained on Desktop , Console , and Mobile , by purchasing it from the Dryad in Hardmode for 2 SC 50 CC each. It is considered a safe wall when placed by the player.

notes: Hallowed Seeds are Hardmode seeds used to plant Hallowed grass on Dirt Blocks , which will then spread The Hallow . Hallowed Seeds are purchased from the Dryad in Hardmode for 20 SC each.

The Hammush is a Hardmode , post-one mechanical boss hammer that is purchased from the Truffle for 40 GC . When swung, the Hammush releases mushroom projectiles at its tip. Like the Pwnhammer , it can destroy altars .

conditions: Hay is a type of block that is harvested from wild grass plants and sea oats by using a Sickle . It can be used to make Target Dummies .
